---
title: "Machine Learning in Python"
subtitle: "From Beginner to Advanced: From Scratch"
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

::: {.hero-banner}
# 🐍 Machine Learning in Python

**A comprehensive tutorial series covering theory, mathematics, and pure-Python implementation of every major ML algorithm — no scikit-learn, no TensorFlow.**

::: {.badge-row}
[🟢 Beginner]{.badge-beginner} [🟡 Intermediate]{.badge-intermediate} [🔴 Advanced]{.badge-advanced} [21 Chapters]{.badge-all} [Pure Python]{.badge-all}
:::
:::

## What You'll Learn

This tutorial takes you from the mathematical foundations of machine learning all the way through deep learning and reinforcement learning, with **every algorithm coded from scratch in plain Python** (no NumPy for core algorithms, no ML libraries).

Each chapter follows a consistent structure:

1. **Intuition** — Plain-English motivation
2. **Theory** — Mathematical formulation with notation explained
3. **Algorithm** — Step-by-step pseudocode
4. **From-Scratch Python** — Complete, runnable implementation
5. **Exercises** — Practice problems

---

## 🟢 Beginner Path

::: {.chapter-grid}

::: {.chapter-card}
[Chapter 00]{.card-number}
[Prerequisites & Setup]{.card-title}
[Python math utilities, vector/matrix operations, data normalization — the toolkit we use in every chapter.]{.card-desc}
::: {.card-footer}
[🟢 Beginner]{.badge-beginner} · 2–3 hrs
:::
[→ Start Here](chapters/chapter-00-prerequisites.qmd)
:::

::: {.chapter-card}
[Chapter 01]{.card-number}
[Introduction to Machine Learning]{.card-title}
[Types of ML, the learning workflow, key terminology, bias-variance tradeoff, and your first baseline model.]{.card-desc}
::: {.card-footer}
[🟢 Beginner]{.badge-beginner} · 1–2 hrs
:::
[→ Read Chapter](chapters/chapter-01-intro-to-ml.qmd)
:::

::: {.chapter-card}
[Chapter 02]{.card-number}
[Linear Regression]{.card-title}
[Ordinary least squares, gradient descent, MSE cost function, R² evaluation. The foundation of supervised learning.]{.card-desc}
::: {.card-footer}
[🟢 Beginner]{.badge-beginner} · 3–4 hrs
:::
[→ Read Chapter](chapters/chapter-02-linear-regression.qmd)
:::

::: {.chapter-card}
[Chapter 03]{.card-number}
[Logistic Regression]{.card-title}
[Sigmoid function, binary cross-entropy loss, gradient descent for classification. One-vs-rest multiclass extension.]{.card-desc}
::: {.card-footer}
[🟢 Beginner]{.badge-beginner} · 3–4 hrs
:::
[→ Read Chapter](chapters/chapter-03-logistic-regression.qmd)
:::

::: {.chapter-card}
[Chapter 04]{.card-number}
[K-Nearest Neighbors]{.card-title}
[Distance metrics, lazy learning, KNN for classification and regression, curse of dimensionality.]{.card-desc}
::: {.card-footer}
[🟢 Beginner]{.badge-beginner} · 2–3 hrs
:::
[→ Read Chapter](chapters/chapter-04-knn.qmd)
:::

::: {.chapter-card}
[Chapter 05]{.card-number}
[Naive Bayes]{.card-title}
[Bayes' theorem, Gaussian NB for continuous data, Multinomial NB for text, Laplace smoothing, spam detection.]{.card-desc}
::: {.card-footer}
[🟢 Beginner]{.badge-beginner} · 3 hrs
:::
[→ Read Chapter](chapters/chapter-05-naive-bayes.qmd)
:::

:::

---

## 🟡 Intermediate Path

::: {.chapter-grid}

::: {.chapter-card}
[Chapter 06]{.card-number}
[Decision Trees]{.card-title}
[Entropy, Gini impurity, information gain, CART algorithm for classification and regression.]{.card-desc}
::: {.card-footer}
[🟡 Intermediate]{.badge-intermediate} · 4–5 hrs
:::
[→ Read Chapter](chapters/chapter-06-decision-trees.qmd)
:::

::: {.chapter-card}
[Chapter 07]{.card-number}
[Support Vector Machines]{.card-title}
[Maximum margin hyperplane, soft-margin SVM, hinge loss, kernel trick (RBF, polynomial).]{.card-desc}
::: {.card-footer}
[🟡 Intermediate]{.badge-intermediate} · 4–5 hrs
:::
[→ Read Chapter](chapters/chapter-07-svm.qmd)
:::

::: {.chapter-card}
[Chapter 08]{.card-number}
[Ensemble Methods]{.card-title}
[Bagging, Random Forest, Extra Trees, AdaBoost, Gradient Boosting, and XGBoost — classification and regression from scratch with full theory and benchmarks.]{.card-desc}
::: {.card-footer}
[🟡 Intermediate]{.badge-intermediate} · 6–8 hrs
:::
[→ Read Chapter](chapters/chapter-08-ensemble-methods.qmd)
:::

::: {.chapter-card}
[Chapter 12]{.card-number}
[K-Means Clustering]{.card-title}
[Unsupervised clustering, K-Means++ initialization, elbow method, silhouette score.]{.card-desc}
::: {.card-footer}
[🟡 Intermediate]{.badge-intermediate} · 3 hrs
:::
[→ Read Chapter](chapters/chapter-12-kmeans.qmd)
:::

::: {.chapter-card}
[Chapter 13]{.card-number}
[Principal Component Analysis]{.card-title}
[Covariance matrix, eigendecomposition via power iteration, dimensionality reduction, explained variance.]{.card-desc}
::: {.card-footer}
[🟡 Intermediate]{.badge-intermediate} · 4–5 hrs
:::
[→ Read Chapter](chapters/chapter-13-pca.qmd)
:::

:::

---

## 🔴 Advanced Path

::: {.chapter-grid}

::: {.chapter-card}
[Chapter 09]{.card-number}
[Neural Networks (MLP)]{.card-title}
[Perceptrons, forward pass, backpropagation, activation functions, He initialization, XOR problem.]{.card-desc}
::: {.card-footer}
[🔴 Advanced]{.badge-advanced} · 6–8 hrs
:::
[→ Read Chapter](chapters/chapter-09-neural-networks.qmd)
:::

::: {.chapter-card}
[Chapter 10]{.card-number}
[Convolutional Neural Networks]{.card-title}
[2D convolution, padding, stride, max pooling, feature maps, Sobel edge detection from scratch.]{.card-desc}
::: {.card-footer}
[🔴 Advanced]{.badge-advanced} · 6–8 hrs
:::
[→ Read Chapter](chapters/chapter-10-cnn.qmd)
:::

::: {.chapter-card}
[Chapter 11]{.card-number}
[Recurrent Neural Networks]{.card-title}
[Vanilla RNN, vanishing gradient problem, LSTM gates (forget/input/output/cell), GRU architecture.]{.card-desc}
::: {.card-footer}
[🔴 Advanced]{.badge-advanced} · 6–8 hrs
:::
[→ Read Chapter](chapters/chapter-11-rnn.qmd)
:::

::: {.chapter-card}
[Chapter 14]{.card-number}
[Reinforcement Learning]{.card-title}
[Markov decision processes, Q-learning, Bellman equation, ε-greedy policy, GridWorld agent.]{.card-desc}
::: {.card-footer}
[🔴 Advanced]{.badge-advanced} · 5–6 hrs
:::
[→ Read Chapter](chapters/chapter-14-reinforcement-learning.qmd)
:::

::: {.chapter-card}
[Chapter 15]{.card-number}
[Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM)]{.card-title}
[Gates (forget/input/output), cell state highway, reparameterised gradients, GRU comparison, character-level language model.]{.card-desc}
::: {.card-footer}
[🔴 Advanced]{.badge-advanced} · 6–8 hrs
:::
[→ Read Chapter](chapters/chapter-15-lstm.qmd)
:::

::: {.chapter-card}
[Chapter 16]{.card-number}
[CNNs — Advanced Architectures]{.card-title}
[Batch normalisation, residual (skip) connections, depthwise separable conv, IoU, non-maximum suppression, data augmentation.]{.card-desc}
::: {.card-footer}
[🔴 Advanced]{.badge-advanced} · 6–8 hrs
:::
[→ Read Chapter](chapters/chapter-16-cnn-advanced.qmd)
:::

::: {.chapter-card}
[Chapter 17]{.card-number}
[Graph Neural Networks]{.card-title}
[Message passing, GCN spectral convolution, GraphSAGE, link prediction, graph pooling, node classification.]{.card-desc}
::: {.card-footer}
[🔴 Advanced]{.badge-advanced} · 6–8 hrs
:::
[→ Read Chapter](chapters/chapter-17-gnn.qmd)
:::

::: {.chapter-card}
[Chapter 18]{.card-number}
[Natural Language Processing]{.card-title}
[Tokenisation, BPE, TF-IDF, Word2Vec skip-gram, scaled dot-product attention, positional encoding, Transformer encoder.]{.card-desc}
::: {.card-footer}
[🔴 Advanced]{.badge-advanced} · 8–10 hrs
:::
[→ Read Chapter](chapters/chapter-18-nlp.qmd)
:::

::: {.chapter-card}
[Chapter 19]{.card-number}
[Generative Models]{.card-title}
[VAE (ELBO, reparameterisation trick), GAN (min-max game, mode collapse), diffusion models (forward/reverse process).]{.card-desc}
::: {.card-footer}
[🔴 Advanced]{.badge-advanced} · 8–10 hrs
:::
[→ Read Chapter](chapters/chapter-19-generative-models.qmd)
:::

:::

---

## 📊 Best Practices

::: {.chapter-grid}

::: {.chapter-card}
[Chapter 20]{.card-number}
[Model Evaluation & Best Practices]{.card-title}
[Cross-validation, confusion matrix, ROC-AUC, regression metrics, overfitting/regularization, learning curves.]{.card-desc}
::: {.card-footer}
[🔵 All Levels]{.badge-all} · 3–4 hrs
:::
[→ Read Chapter](chapters/chapter-20-model-evaluation.qmd)
:::

:::

---

## 🛠️ Philosophy

> "The best way to understand an algorithm is to implement it from scratch."

This tutorial uses **only Python's standard library** for all core algorithm implementations.  
No `sklearn`, no `torch`, no `tensorflow` — just pure Python logic and `math`.

This forces you to truly understand what each algorithm is doing, not just call `.fit()`.

---

## 🗺️ Suggested Learning Paths

| Path | Chapters | Who it's for |
|------|----------|-------------|
| 🟢 **Beginner** | 00 → 01 → 02 → 03 → 04 → 05 → 20 | New to ML |
| 🟡 **Intermediate** | 00–05 → 06 → 07 → 08 → 12 → 13 → 20 | Comfortable with Python |
| 🔴 **Deep Learning** | 09 → 10 → 11 → 14 → 15 → 16 → 20 | Neural network focus |
| 🔴 **Modern AI** | 15 → 16 → 17 → 18 → 19 → 20 | LSTM, GNNs, NLP, Generative |
| 🔴 **Full Course** | 00 → 20 in order | Complete mastery |
